Representing more than 40% of global GDP, these industries are the infrastructure of our planet, including agriculture, construction, field services, transportation, and manufacturing — and we are excited to help digitally transform their operations at scale.</span></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 300; font-family: arial, helvetica, sans-serif;">Working at Samsara means you’ll help define the future of physical operations and be on a team that’s shaping an exciting array of product solutions, including Video-Based Safety, Vehicle Telematics, Apps and Driver Workflows, and Equipment Monitoring. As part of a recently public company, you’ll have the autonomy and support to make an impact as we build for the long term.</span></p></div><p><strong>About the role:</strong></p> <p>The Manager will oversee a team of Technical Account Managers (TAMs) and is instrumental in ensuring the delivery of exceptional technical support and account management. This leader will act as a champion of change, utilizing a data-driven approach to decision-making and process optimization to drive long-term customer success. You will be accountable for team metrics, fostering professional development, and maintaining alignment with Samsara's core values</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>You should apply if:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>You want to impact the industries that run our world:</strong> Your efforts will result in real-world impact – helping to keep the lights on, get food into grocery stores, reduce emissions, and most importantly, ensure workers return home safely.</li> <li><strong>You are the architect of your own career:</strong> If you put in the work, this role won’t be your last at Samsara. We set up our employees for success and have built a culture that encourages rapid career development, countless opportunities to experiment and master your craft in a hyper growth environment.</li> <li><strong>You’re energized by our opportunity:</strong> The vision we have to digitize large sectors of the global economy requires your full focus and best efforts to bring forth creative, ambitious ideas for our customers.</li> <li><strong>You want to be with the best:</strong> At Samsara, we win together, celebrate together and support each other. You<strong> </strong>will be surrounded by a high-calibre team that will encourage you to do your best.&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><a href="https://www.samsara.com/blog/what-we-value-at-samsara/"><em>Click here</em></a><em> to learn about what we value at Samsara.&nbsp;</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>This is a remote position open to candidates residing in the US except Alaska, Austin Metro, Boulder Metro, California, Chicago Metro, Connecticut, Dallas Metro, Denver Metro, Houston Metro, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Jersey, New York, Rhode Island, Seattle Metro, and Washington, D.C.</strong></p> <p>In this role, you will focus on building excellence and scaling our impact through the following:</p> <h3><strong>Build a High-Performance Culture</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Inclusive Talent Strategy:</strong> <strong>&nbsp;</strong>Hire, develop and lead an inclusive, engaged, and high performing team</li> <li><strong>Performance Excellence:</strong> Establish a coaching rhythm and clear performance benchmarks to ensure the team consistently meets or exceeds targets.</li> <li><strong>Career Architecture:</strong> Actively engage in professional growth through tailored development plans and mentorship, ensuring a robust internal pipeline for career progression.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Engineer Scalable Processes</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Intelligence Scalability:</strong> Develop and deploy AI-assisted workflows, such as automated technical health checks and AI-summarized account reviews to make it easier for the team to manage complex enterprise accounts.&nbsp;</li> <li><strong>Cross-Functional Influence:</strong> Partner closely with Sales, Product, and Customer Success to bridge the gap between customer feedback and product evolution, driving continuous service improvements.</li> <li><strong>Strategic Roadmap:</strong> Contribute to the long-term strategic planning required to scale a TAM team in a hyper-growth environment.</li> </ul> <h3><strong>Drive Data-Led Change</strong></h3> <ul> <li><strong>Analytical Leadership:</strong> Apply a rigorous, data-driven approach to identify organizational risks, dependencies, and hidden opportunities for efficiency.</li> <li><strong>Platform Advocacy:</strong> Translate technical and business insights into actionable data to enhance the Samsara platform experience for our largest enterprise customers.</li> <li><strong>Escalation Management:</strong> Serve as the primary point of contact for high-priority technical escalations, using deep product knowledge and analytical problem-solving to ensure swift, permanent resolutions.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Minimum requirements for the role:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>People Management:</strong> Minimum of 3 years in a people manager role with a proven track record of leading globally distributed technical account managers through periods of significant change.</li> <li><strong>Enterprise Impact:</strong> Demonstrated success in achieving technical account management goals and maintaining high retention for enterprise-level customers in a fast-paced environment.</li> <li><strong>Change Advocacy &amp; Influence:</strong> Exceptional skills in influencing all organizational levels, with the ability to translate complex technical data into clear business cases for executive stakeholders.</li> <li><strong>Foundational Education:</strong> Bachelor's degree in a technical or business field, or equivalent practical work experience</li> </ul> <p><strong>An ideal candidate also has:</strong></p> <ul> <li><strong>Technical Depth:</strong> Demonstrated expertise in IoT, APIs, SQL, and scripting languages, allowing you to act as a credible mentor and technical sounding board for the team.</li> <li><strong>Scalability Mindset:</strong> Prior experience scaling TAM teams or technical service organizations, specifically focusing on building additional services and repeatable service models.</li> <li><strong>Data Fluency:</strong> Advanced proficiency with Gainsight, Tableau, Salesforce, or Zendesk to build dashboards, identify trends, and drive proactive, data-led strategies.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Outcome-Driven Business Acumen:</strong> A strong focus on driving value-based outcomes and ROI for customers rather than just managing support tickets or task completion.</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><span style="font-size: 16px;">The range of annual base salary for full-time employees for this position is below. Please note that base pay offered may vary depending on factors including your city of residence, job-related knowledge, skills, and experience. Learn more about our total rewards and benefits below.</span></p></div><div class="title">Annual Base Salary</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$85,977.50</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$115,600 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><p><strong>Total Rewards</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we build for the people who keep the global economy moving. We want owners, not passengers, which is why <a href="https://www.samsara.com/company/careers/benefits">our rewards</a> are designed to fuel high-impact builders. Our compensation program delivers above-market total compensation through a combination of base salary, performance-based bonus/variable pay, and equity (for eligible roles) in a high-growth public company. We meaningfully differentiate pay for our top performers, who have the opportunity to earn above-market compensation that can outpace the broader market over time.</p> <p>Beyond compensation, we provide the foundations that enable long-term success: a flexible, employee-led remote model, a professional development stipend, comprehensive health and parental leave plans, and more. If you’re ready to build for the long term and own the outcome, your journey starts here.</p> <p><strong>Flexible Working&nbsp;</strong></p> <p><span style="font-weight: 400;">At Samsara, we embrace a flexible working model that caters to the diverse needs of our teams. Our offices are open for those who prefer to work in-person and we also support remote work where it aligns with our operational requirements. For certain positions, being close to one of our offices or within a specific geographic area is important to facilitate collaboration, access to resources, or alignment with our service regions. In these cases, the job description will clearly indicate any working location requirements. Our goal is to ensure that all members of our team can contribute effectively, whether they are working on-site, in a hybrid model, or fully remotely. All offers of employment are contingent upon an individual’s ability to secure and maintain the legal right to work at the company and in the specified work location, if applicable.</span></p> <p><strong>Belonging at Samsara</strong></p> <p>At Samsara, we welcome everyone regardless of their background. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, protected veteran status, disability, age, and other characteristics protected by law. We depend on the unique approaches of our team members to help us solve complex problems and want to ensure that Samsara is a place where people from all backgrounds can make an impact.</p> <p><strong>Accommodations&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Samsara is an inclusive work environment, and we are committed to ensuring equal opportunity in employment for qualified persons with disabilities. Please email <a href="mailto:accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com">accessibleinterviewing@samsara.com</a> or <a href="https://forms.gle/DrM6DFQUiSgfPtCVA">click here</a> if you require any reasonable accommodations throughout the recruiting process.</p> <p><strong>Our Commitment to Authenticity</strong></p> <p>We use <a href="https://hiretofu.com/privacyPolicy">Tofu</a>, a fraud detection tool, to validate the authenticity of applications and protect against identity fraud. This ensures we are connecting with real people and allows us to prioritize genuine candidates. Please see Samsara’s <a href="https://www.samsara.com/legal/candidate-privacy-notice">Candidate Privacy Notice</a> for more information.</p> <p><strong>Fraudulent Employment Offers</strong></p> <p>Samsara is aware of scams involving fake job interviews and offers. Please know we do not charge fees to applicants at any stage of the hiring process. Official communication about your application will only come from emails ending in @samsara.com, @us-greenhouse-mail.io or @mail3.guide.co. For more information regarding fraudulent employment offers, please visit our&nbsp;<a href="https://samsara.com/blog/avoiding-fake-job-offers">blog post here</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Commercial Account Executive, Mid-Market - US West
jobs.description: <div class="content-intro"><p>GitLab is the intelligent orchestration platform for DevSecOps. GitLab enables organizations to increase developer productivity, improve operational efficiency, reduce security and compliance risk, and accelerate digital transformation. More than 50 million registered users and more than 50% of the Fortune 100* trust GitLab to ship better, more secure software faster.</p> <p>The same principles built into our products are reflected in how our team works: we embrace AI as a core productivity multiplier, with all team members expected to incorporate AI into their daily workflows to drive efficiency, innovation, and impact. GitLab is where careers accelerate, innovation flourishes, and every voice is valued. Our high-performance culture is driven by our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/values/">values</a> and continuous knowledge exchange, enabling our team members to reach their full potential while collaborating with industry leaders to solve complex problems. <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OuZIb5zszQI">Co-create the future with us</a> as we build technology that transforms how the world develops software.</p> <p>*<em>Fortune 500® is a registered trademark of Fortune Media IP Limited, used under license. Claim based on GitLab data. Fortune 100 refers to the top 20% ranked companies in the 2025 Fortune 500 list, published in June 2025. Fortune and Fortune Media IP Limited are not affiliated with, and do not endorse products or services of GitLab.</em></p></div><h2><strong>An overview of this role</strong></h2> <p>As a Commercial Account Executive, Mid-Market, you’ll be the primary point of contact for prospective and existing customers in our mid-market segment, helping organizations modernize how they plan, build, secure, and ship software with GitLab’s AI-powered DevSecOps platform. You’ll own a broad book of business in your territory and serve as a strategic, trusted advisor to your accounts as you prospect, run complex sales cycles, and guide customers on their journey with GitLab to achieve clear business outcomes while supporting adoption and reducing churn. You’ll collaborate closely with Sales Development, Solutions Consultant, Renewals, Partners, and Sales leadership, using value-based selling and structured sales methodologies (such as MEDDPICC and Command of the Message) to build a healthy pipeline, run accurate forecasts, and support revenue goals across a wide range of deal sizes.</p> <p>Must be located in Central, Mountain, or Pacific Timezones in the US.&nbsp;</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <h2><strong>What you’ll do</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Own and manage a broad Mid-Market book of business as the primary point of contact in your territory, focusing on new and expansion opportunities.</li> <li>Focus on customer accounts with 250 to 1,999 employees, managing the spectrum of project sizes and complexity within the mid-market segment.</li> <li>Articulate the value of GitLab’s AI-powered DevSecOps platform to Mid-Market prospects and customers, tailoring messaging to their business and technical needs.</li> <li>Build and maintain a healthy pipeline through consistent prospecting, qualification, and advancement of opportunities, using structured methodologies (such as MEDDPICC and Command of the Message) to support your pipeline and revenue goals.</li> <li>Document buying criteria, decision processes, next steps, and key stakeholders to run disciplined, well-orchestrated sales cycles.</li> <li>Collaborate with sales development, customer success, renewals, marketing, technical teams, and partners to deliver a cohesive pre- and post-sales experience, co-sell and close opportunities, and expand GitLab’s footprint while delivering added value for customers.</li> <li>Support customer adoption of GitLab solutions, proactively work to reduce churn and contraction, and participate in quarterly forecasting and territory planning.</li> <li>Contribute to continuous improvement by sharing win/loss insights, updating the sales handbook, and being a clear voice of the customer into the product via our public issue tracker.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>What you’ll bring</strong></h2> <ul> <li>Experience selling SaaS solutions by leading value-based conversations with technical and business stakeholders, ideally including development or DevSecOps teams.</li> <li>Ability to manage a broad mid-market territory as the primary point of contact, owning a book of business from prospecting through close, adoption, and expansion.</li> <li>Skill in building and maintaining strong customer relationships, including effective discovery, negotiation, and closing to support long-term customer outcomes.</li> <li>Comfortable driving consistent outbound prospecting activity, creating and maintaining a healthy pipeline, and maintaining accurate territory and forecast documentation.</li> <li>Ability to collaborate closely with cross-functional partners (sales development, customer success, renewals, marketing, partners, and technical teams) to coordinate account strategies.</li> <li>Clear and structured communication skills, including presenting the GitLab value proposition to different audience levels and documenting customer insights and lessons learned.</li> <li>Interest in GitLab, open source, and tools that support software development, with the ability to connect customer business challenges to GitLab’s DevSecOps platform.</li> <li>Willingness to travel to meet customers and attend events as needed, and openness to bringing relevant, transferable sales experience from a variety of backgrounds.</li> </ul> <h2><strong>About the team</strong></h2> <p>The Commercial Account Executive - Mid-Market role sits within GitLab’s broader Sales organization, focused on helping mid-market customers adopt and expand their use of our AI powered DevSecOps platform. You’ll be part of a distributed team of Account Executives, Sales Development, Solution Consultants, Renewals, and Partners who work asynchronously across regions to manage a broad book of business and a wide range of opportunity sizes. Our mission is to guide customers through their journey with GitLab, from first evaluation to long term value realization, by developing new business, strengthening customer relationships, and supporting healthy product adoption. We collaborate closely through shared playbooks, our sales handbook, and GitLab itself to stay aligned on territory plans, pipeline health, and best practices. For more on how we work, see the<a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/job-families/sales/account-executive/#commercial-account-executive---mid-market"> Commercial Account Executive Handbook</a>.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>#LI-SK1</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p><em>The base salary range for this role’s listed level is currently for residents of the&nbsp;United States only. This range is intended to reflect the role's base salary rate in locations throughout the US.&nbsp;Grade level and salary ranges are determined through interviews and a review of education, experience, knowledge, skills, abilities of the applicant, equity with other team members, alignment with market data,&nbsp;and geographic location.&nbsp;The base salary range does not include any bonuses, equity, or benefits. See more information on our <a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLBeneUS" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">benefits</a><span data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#000000;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:none;font-family:'Arial';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;"> and </span><a class="waffle-rich-text-link" href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/stock-options/" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-link="https://bit.ly/GLequity" data-sheets-formula-bar-text-style="font-size:12px;color:#1155cc;font-weight:normal;text-decoration:underline;font-family:''Arial'';font-style:normal;text-decoration-skip-ink:none;">equity</a>. Sales roles are also eligible for incentive pay targeted at up to 100% of the offered base salary.</em></p></div><div class="title">United States Salary Range</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$66,300</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$117,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><h4><strong>How GitLab Supports Full-Time Employees</strong></h4> <ul> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/">Benefits to support your health, finances, and well-being</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/paid-time-off/">Flexible Paid Time Off</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/erg-guide/#:~:text=Definition%20of%20the%20TMRG%20%2D%20Team%20Member%20Resource%20Groups,-TMRGs%20are%20voluntary&amp;text=The%20purpose%20for%20this%20type,developing%20the%20sense%20of%20belonging.">Team Member Resource Groups</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/stock-options/">Equity Compensation &amp; Employee Stock Purchase Plan</a></li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/people-group/learning-and-development/growth-and-development/">Growth and Development Fund</a></li> <li><a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/total-rewards/benefits/general-and-entity-benefits/#parental-leave">Parental leave</a>&nbsp;</li> <li><a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/finance/expenses/#equipment">Home office</a> support</li> </ul> <p>Please note that we welcome interest from candidates with varying levels of experience; many successful candidates do not meet every single requirement. Additionally, studies have shown that people from <a href="https://about.gitlab.com/company/culture/inclusion/#examples-of-select-underrepresented-groups">underrepresented groups</a> are less likely to apply to a job unless they meet every single qualification. If you're excited about this role, please apply and allow our recruiters to assess your application.</p> <hr> <p><strong>Country Hiring Guidelines: </strong>GitLab hires new team members in countries around the world. All of our roles are remote, however some roles may carry specific location-based eligibility requirements. Our Talent Acquisition team can help answer any questions about location after starting the recruiting process.&nbsp;&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Privacy Policy: </strong>Please review our <a href="https://handbook.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/candidate-faq/recruitment-privacy-policy/" target="_blank">Recruitment Privacy Policy.</a> Your privacy is important to us.</p> <p>GitLab is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace and is an affirmative action employer. GitLab’s policies and practices relating to recruitment, employment, career development and advancement, promotion, and retirement are based solely on merit, regardless of race, color, religion, ancestry, sex (including pregnancy, lactation, sexual orientation, gender identity, or gender expression), national origin, age, citizenship, marital status, mental or physical disability, genetic information (including family medical history), discharge status from the military, protected veteran status (which includes disabled veterans, recently separated veterans, active duty wartime or campaign badge veterans, and Armed Forces service medal veterans), or any other basis protected by law. GitLab will not tolerate discrimination or harassment based on any of these characteristics. See also&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#equal-employment-opportunity-policy" target="_blank">GitLab’s EEO Policy</a>&nbsp;and&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/labor-and-employment-notices/#eeoc-us-equal-employment-opportunity-commission-notices" target="_blank">EEO is the Law</a>. If you have a disability or special need that requires&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/people-policies/inc-usa/#reasonable-accommodation" target="_blank">accommodation</a>, please let us know during the&nbsp;<a href="https://about.gitlab.com/handbook/hiring/interviewing/#adjustments-to-our-interview-process" target="_blank">recruiting process</a>.</p></div>

jobs.title: Software Engineer, Credit Risk Platform
jobs.description: <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>About the role</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Chime Engineering is growing rapidly as we scale to support the financial needs of our members. We are looking for driven engineers to join our Credit Risk Platform team, where you’ll build backend services and internal tools that help Chime manage credit risk and losses across all of our credit products, including Loans, MyPay, SpotMe, and a growing pipeline of new credit products in development.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">This team is building a shared risk platform that expands with every new product we launch. A key part of the mission is being able to onboard new credit products as a platform capability, not a reinvention of decisioning, controls, and monitoring each time. You’ll partner closely with Credit Risk, Product, Analytics, and Data Science to help risk experts iterate safely using better tooling, richer data, and clear guardrails. That includes integrating third-party data sources and building systems that are reliable, observable, and auditable, because they sit on the critical path of member experiences and credit outcomes.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">The base salary offered for this role and level of experience will begin at $133,000 and up to $184,000. Full-time employees are also eligible for a bonus, competitive equity package, and benefits. The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location, skills, qualifications, and experience.&nbsp;</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>In this role, you can expect to</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Design, develop, test, and maintain backend services and internal tools that support risk strategy across multiple products (e.g., eligibility, limits, exposure tracking, policy enforcement)</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Contribute to reusable platform primitives that make it faster and safer to launch new credit products without duplicating risk decisioning and controls</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Build and improve workflows that enable Credit Risk teams to adjust strategies (rules, thresholds, segmentation) with appropriate guardrails and review processes</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Integrate third-party data sources used in decisioning and monitoring, including implementing data validation, error handling, and resilience to vendor changes</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Add instrumentation, dashboards, and alerts for key risk and loss signals, and participate in on-call/incident response as part of a supportive team rotation</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Collaborate with senior engineers to learn best practices in system design, testing, and operational excellence</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Proactively identify reliability and scalability improvements and drive them to completion with mentorship and feedback</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>To thrive in this role, you have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">3+ years developing software in a production environment</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience with Ruby on Rails or comparable frameworks, building and operating APIs</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience working with relational databases and caching systems, and writing safe schema changes and migrations</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Familiarity with distributed systems fundamentals (async processing, idempotency, retries, backfills) and an interest in learning deeper system design</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">A strong appreciation for maintainable, well-tested code and a willingness to improve existing systems, not just ship new ones</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Comfort working across teams to clarify requirements, communicate tradeoffs, and deliver incrementally</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Interest in data-driven systems and integrating external/third-party data sources (fintech or risk experience is a plus, not required)</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">#LI-Hybrid #LI-SB1</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>A little about us</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">At Chime, we believe that everyone can achieve financial progress. We created Chime—a financial technology company, not a bank*—on the premise that core banking services should be helpful, easy, and free. Through our user-friendly tools and intuitive platforms, we empower our members to take control of their finances and work towards their goals. Whether it's starting a savings account, purchasing a first car or home, launching a business, or pursuing higher education, we're proud to have helped millions unlock their financial potential.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We're a team of problem solvers, dreamers, and builders with one shared obsession: our members. From day one, Chimers have worked tirelessly to out-hustle and out-execute competitors to bring our mission to life. Their grit and determination inspire us to work harder every day to deliver the very best experience possible. We each bring an owner's mindset to our work, refusing to be outdone and holding ourselves accountable to meet and exceed the highest bars for our teams, our company, and our members.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We believe in being bold, dreaming big, and taking risks, while also working together, embracing our diverse perspectives, and giving each other honest feedback. Our culture remains deeply entrepreneurial, encouraging every Chimer to see themselves as stewards of our mission to help everyday Americans unlock their financial progress.&nbsp;</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We know that to achieve our mission, we must earn and keep people's trust—so we hold ourselves to the highest standards of integrity in everything we do. These aren't just words on a wall—our values are embedded in every aspect of our business, serving as a north star that guides us as we work to help millions achieve their financial potential.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Because if we don't—who will?</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">*Chime is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services provided by The Bancorp Bank, N.A. or Stride Bank, N.A., Members FDIC.</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>What we offer for our full-time, regular employees</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">🏢 Our in-office work policy is designed to keep you connected - with four days a week in the office and Fridays from home for those near one of our offices, plus team and company-wide events depending on location. Whether you’re coming in regularly or are part of our fully remote program, you’ll stay engaged with your work and teammates.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">💻 In-office perks including backup child, elder, and/or pet care, plus a subsidized commuter benefit to support your regular commute</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">💰 Competitive salary based on experience</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">✨ 401k match plus great medical, dental, vision, life, and disability benefits</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">🏝 Generous vacation policy and company-wide Chime Days, bonus company-wide paid days off</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">🫂 1% of your time off to support local community organizations of your choice</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">👟 Annual wellness stipend to use towards eligible wellness related expenses</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">👶 Up to 24 weeks of paid parental leave for birthing parents and 12 weeks of paid parental leave for non-birthing parents</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">👪 Access to Maven, a family planning tool, with $15k lifetime reimbursement for egg freezing, fertility treatments, adoption, and more.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">🎉 In-person and virtual events to connect with your fellow Chimers—think cooking classes, guided meditations, music festivals, mixology classes, paint nights, etc., and delicious snack boxes, too!</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">💚 A challenging and fulfilling opportunity to join one of the most experienced teams in FinTech and help millions unlock financial progress</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We know that great work can’t be done without a diverse team and inclusive environment. That’s why we specifically look for individuals of varying strengths, skills, backgrounds, and ideas to join our team. We believe this gives us a competitive advantage to better serve our members and helps us all grow as Chimers and individuals.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Chime is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We consider qualified applicants without regard to race, color, ancestry, religion, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, age, marital or family status, disability, genetic information, veteran status, or any other legally protected basis under provincial, federal, state, and local laws, regulations, or ordinances. We will also consider qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with the requirements of state and local laws, including the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, Cook County Ordinance, NYC Fair Chance Act, and the LA City Fair Chance Ordinance, and consistent with Canadian provincial and federal laws. jobs.title: Software Engineer, Instant Loans
jobs.description: <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>About the role</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Chime’s Instant Loans team builds products that give members upfront liquidity for larger, specific needs, with clear, predictable repayment that helps them build credit responsibly. As an engineer on the team, you’ll work across the stack to build and scale services that offer a few hundred to a few thousand dollars over terms from months to years, making fair credit accessible to people who are often denied by traditional banks. This role is ideal for engineers who want to move quickly, collaborate closely with product, design, data, and risk, and care deeply about expanding financial access and stability for millions of members.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">The base salary offered for this role and level of experience will begin at $133,000 and up to $184,000. Full-time employees are also eligible for a bonus, competitive equity package, and benefits. The actual base salary offered may be higher, depending on your location, skills, qualifications, and experience.&nbsp;</span></p> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>In this role, you can expect to</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Design, develop, test, refactor and scale backend implementations of new and existing consumer product features</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Work on member-facing mobile app, including designing and building strong, resilient APIs that power key member experiences.</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Collaborate with cross functional teams to understand member pain points and come up with solutions</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Develop thoroughly tested code and conduct quality assurance on all new code alongside our QA team</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Proactively identify opportunities for improving the reliability and scalability of our systems</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Engineers are required to participate in on-call rotation. Being on call may include responding to incidents outside of regular working hours when necessary.</span></li> </ul> <h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>To thrive in this role, you have</strong></span></h2> <ul> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">3+ years developing software in a production environment at scale</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Proven experience with Ruby on Rails or comparable frameworks</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Comfort working across the stack and an eagerness to learn new technologies</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience designing APIs, working with transactional databases and caching systems</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">A product mindset with a strong bias toward experimentation, iteration, and measuring impact</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Exposure to large-scale architecture patterns and an appreciation for clean, maintainable code</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">A collaborative spirit and strong communication skills, with experience working cross-functionally</span></li> <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">Experience working in a fast-paced, product-focused environment (Fintech experience is a plus)</span></li> </ul> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">#LI-Hybrid #LI-SB1</span></p><div class="content-conclusion"><h2><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><strong>A little about us</strong></span></h2> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">At Chime, we believe that everyone can achieve financial progress. We created Chime—a financial technology company, not a bank*—on the premise that core banking services should be helpful, easy, and free. Through our user-friendly tools and intuitive platforms, we empower our members to take control of their finances and work towards their goals. Whether it's starting a savings account, purchasing a first car or home, launching a business, or pursuing higher education, we're proud to have helped millions unlock their financial potential.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We're a team of problem solvers, dreamers, and builders with one shared obsession: our members. From day one, Chimers have worked tirelessly to out-hustle and out-execute competitors to bring our mission to life. Their grit and determination inspire us to work harder every day to deliver the very best experience possible. We each bring an owner's mindset to our work, refusing to be outdone and holding ourselves accountable to meet and exceed the highest bars for our teams, our company, and our members.</span></p> <p><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">We believe in being bold, dreaming big, and taking risks, while also working together, embracing our diverse perspectives, and giving each other honest feedback. helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">🏝 Generous vacation policy and company-wide Chime Days, bonus company-wide paid days off</span></li> <li <li style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;"><span style="font-family: helvetica, arial, sans-serif;">👟 Annual wellness stipend to use towards eligible wellness related expenses</span></li> jobs.title: AI Research Scientist – Datadog AI Research (DAIR)
jobs.description: <p>As a Research Scientist on our team, you will partner with Research Engineers, working on fundamental research problems and collaborating with Datadog's product and engineering teams to translate research advances into products.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>Building on our track record of AI-powered solutions (e.g., <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/bits-ai-sre/">Bits AI</a>, <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/self-optimizing-system/">Bits Evolve</a>, and our <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/ai/toto-boom-unleashed/">time series foundation model</a>), Datadog AI Research tackles high-risk, high-reward problems grounded in real-world challenges in cloud observability and security.</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p>We are focused on two research areas:</p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ol> <li><strong>World Models for Observability</strong> -- Training multimodal foundation models that learn the joint dynamics of distributed systems across metrics, traces, logs, topology, and events. These models power advanced forecasting, anomaly detection, root cause analysis, counterfactual simulation ("what if?"), and provide a learned planning backbone for our autonomous agents.</li> <li><strong>Trained Agents for Observability </strong>-- Post-training models to operate autonomously across Datadog's domain. SRE incident response is our first target, with a clear path to code repair, security response, and infrastructure optimization. We build the simulation environments, RL training loops, and evaluation infrastructure needed to train agents that match or surpass frontier models at a fraction of the cost.</li> </ol> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You'll Do:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Conduct research in generative AI and machine learning, building specialized foundation models and trained agents for observability</li> <li>Train multimodal models on large-scale, diverse telemetry data (metrics, logs, traces, topology, events) using distributed training infrastructure</li> <li>Design and build simulated environments and RL training loops for on-policy agent training and evaluation</li> <li>Collaborate with cross-functional teams (Product, Engineering) to integrate capabilities like multimodal world modeling and autonomous agents into Datadog's products</li> <li>Stay at the forefront of foundation models, world models, and RL-based agent research</li> <li>Contribute to research publications, present at top-tier conferences (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML), and help open-source key model artifacts and benchmarks</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>You hold a PhD in Computer Science, Machine Learning, or a related field, with deep expertise in areas like generative modeling, world models, AI agents, reinforcement learning, or multimodal learning (or have equivalent experience)</li> <li>You have extensive experience designing and implementing deep learning models and agents, with a strong background in distributed training frameworks (e.g., DeepSpeed, Megatron-LM) and ML libraries (PyTorch)</li> <li>You have a track record of impactful publications at top-tier venues (e.g., NeurIPS, ICLR, ICML, TMLR)</li> <li>You are familiar with efficient training, post-training, and inference techniques for large foundation models</li> <li>You can explain complex models and research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Bonus Points (any of the following):</strong></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Experience bridging research and real-world product applications, especially with large foundation models, world models, or RL-trained agents</li> <li>Passion for pushing the boundaries of AI with a focus on customer impact and scalable deployment</li> <li>Experience writing production data pipelines and applications</li> <li>Hands-on experience with GPU programming and optimization, including CUDA</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: Manager I, Engineering - Alerting Platform
jobs.description: <p>Engineering Managers in the Alerting Platform are responsible for the core systems that define and schedule monitors, create alerts, and ensure the accuracy and timeliness of the end to end alerting data across the platform.<br><br></p> <p>This engineering manager will lead the team that owns the product experience of defining and managing monitors and the critical first step on customers' journey of responding to issues - a "full stack" team, from the UI to the public API.&nbsp;<br><br></p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships that it builds, the creativity it brings to the table, and the collaboration of being together. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our employees can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.<br><br></em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Define and prioritize goals in long term roadmaps with engineering and product leadership</li> <li>Work with team members to drive technical direction in architecture decision making and ensuring good review processes</li> <li>Manage and grow team of varying experience levels, intern to senior engineers, with a focus on impact and career development</li> <li>Partner extensively across engineering and product teams to understand needs and collaborate on solutions</li> <li>Lead by example in handling incidents and on-call responsibilities, ensure reliability is a priority.<br><br></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Built and led teams of 4+ engineers</li> <li>Have owned high-scale customer-facing services that are measured on uptime, performance, and correctness&nbsp;</li> <li>Experience with being on-call, leading incident responses, writing post mortems, and driving remediation efforts</li> <li><em>You have a BS/MS/PhD in a Computer Science, Engineering or related scientific field or equivalent professional experience</em></li> <li>Demonstrated ability to use AI coding tools in day-to-day workflows and validate, critique, and refine AI-generated output</li> <li>You’re motivated to push the boundaries of how AI can improve software engineering best practices and contribute to building AI-enabled products</li> </ul> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. jobs.title: Manager II, Engineering - Source Security
jobs.description: <p><strong>About Datadog</strong></p> <p>We're on a mission to build the best platform in the world for engineers to understand and scale their systems, applications, and teams. We operate at a high scale - trillions of data points per day — providing always-on alerting, metrics visualization, logs, and application tracing for tens of thousands of companies. Our engineering culture values pragmatism, honesty, and simplicity to solve hard problems the right way.</p> <p><strong>The Opportunity</strong></p> <p>We are looking for a manager to lead the Source Security team within our broader SDLC Security org. This team is responsible for evaluating the security properties of every single commit at Datadog using a combination of static analysis and LLM based review systems, as well as the governance of our source code and third party dependency ecosystem. You will own the roadmap for growing our existing agentic reviewers (you can read more about this work <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/malicious-pull-requests/">here</a> and <a href="https://www.datadoghq.com/blog/engineering/stopping-hackerbot-claw-with-bewaire/">here</a>), working to maintain the safety and reliability of our systems while the volume of contributions scales dramatically with our adoption of coding agents. You’ll partner with our developer experience organizations on securing our source code and CI environments, and our product teams on making sure that our security controls scale and that the best practices we develop internally are reflected for our customers as well.</p> <p>You’ll join at an ideal time to make a big impact as the need for robust supply chain security is higher than it’s ever been. Datadog as a platform is growing fast and is used very widely, and the continued and exciting expansion of our product portfolio requires agile thinkers with a deep grounding in security fundamentals.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds, and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Lead a small team, working as a hands on technical contributor while building out the roadmap for evaluating the safety of coding agent output using a combination of static analysis and LLM evaluations.</li> <li>Partner with our Developer Experience teams on the configuration of our source code management solutions, ensuring secure guardrails are in place by default.</li> <li>Explore solutions in the coding agent security space such as sandboxing and hook based monitors.</li> <li>Guide and mentor a team of security and software engineers, emphasizing career development, inclusivity, and high performance.</li> <li>Take a pragmatic, risk-based approach to complex technical problems and work with your team to produce well-defined and actionable plans.</li> <li>Balance your time between management and hands-on technical work.</li> <li>Participate in Datadog’s oncall and incident response efforts.</li> <li>Have a direct impact on users and the overall trust of our platform.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experienced managing an engineering team distributed across multiple timezones.</li> <li>Fluent in one or more modern coding languages (Python, Go, JavaScript, etc.)</li> <li>Familiar with continuous security scanning and fine-tuning SAST rules and methodology, as well as AI based coding tools such as Claude, Codex, and Cursor</li> <li>Familiar with Github, Gitlab, or other modern source code management and CI solutions</li> <li>You are able to work closely with engineering and DevOps teams to integrate security seamlessly into the development process.</li> <li>An excellent communicator and collaborator, able to work effectively through complex technical problems with a diverse group of stakeholders.</li> <li>Comfortable working in a fast paced, high growth environment, and able to adapt strategies and solutions in response to emerging challenges and opportunities.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Passionate about building products that engineers love and believe in the true outcome of DevSecOps</li> <li>Familiar with formal verification, fuzzing, or code correctness methodologies</li> <li>Experience productionizing LLM based or agentic workflows</li> <li>Familiar with product workflows, and building security into the user experience</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. jobs.title: Product Manager II – Risk-Based Alerting (SIEM)
jobs.description: <p>As a Product Manager II for Risk-Based Alerting within Datadog’s Cloud SIEM, you will define and deliver intelligent alerting systems that help security teams focus on the most critical threats. You’ll work at the intersection of security, data, and user experience to reduce alert noise, improve signal quality, and enable faster, more confident incident response. Partnering closely with customers and internal teams, you will shape how risk is modeled, prioritized, and surfaced across the platform. This role offers the opportunity to influence core security workflows and drive meaningful outcomes for security teams operating at scale.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Define and drive the product vision for Risk-Based Alerting, focusing on improving signal quality and reducing alert fatigue for security teams</li> <li>Design and evolve risk scoring frameworks using telemetry, user behavior, threat intelligence, and contextual signals</li> <li>Partner with customers and security practitioners to understand triage workflows and translate insights into product improvements</li> <li>Collaborate with engineering, data science, and security teams to build scalable detection pipelines, enrichment systems, and prioritization models</li> <li>Own the end-to-end product lifecycle, from discovery and requirements through launch, iteration, and long-term strategy</li> <li>Define and track success metrics such as alert fidelity, signal-to-noise ratio, MTTD/MTTR, and product adoption</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>3+ years of product management experience building enterprise software for technical users; experience in security or observability is preferred</li> <li>Familiarity with security operations workflows such as threat detection, incident response, or detection engineering</li> <li>Strong product sense with the ability to balance technical systems thinking and intuitive user experience design</li> <li>Experience working cross-functionally with engineering, data science, and go-to-market teams to deliver impactful products</li> <li>Data-driven and structured in your approach to problem-solving, prioritization, and decision-making</li> <li>Effective communicator, able to translate complex technical concepts into clear value for diverse audiences</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p><strong>Benefits and Growth:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive benefits package</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities</li> <li>Employee-focused best in class onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and cross-departmental buddy program</li> <li>Friendly and inclusive workplace culture</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. Actual compensation is based on factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. In addition, Datadog offers a wide range of best in class, comprehensive and inclusive employee benefits for this role including healthcare, dental, parental planning, and mental health benefits, a 401(k) plan and match, paid time off, fitness reimbursements, and a discounted employee stock purchase plan.</p></div><div class="title">The reasonably estimated yearly salary for this role at Datadog is:</div><div class="pay-range"><span>$165,000</span><span class="divider">&mdash;</span><span>$215,000 USD</span></div></div></div><div class="content-conclusion"><hr> <p><strong>About Datadog:&nbsp;</strong></p> <p>Datadog (NASDAQ: DDOG) is a global SaaS business, delivering a rare combination of growth and profitability. We are on a mission to break down silos and solve complexity in the cloud age by enabling digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stacks. Built by engineers, for engineers, Datadog is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</span></em></p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Partner with the sales team to articulate the overall Datadog value proposition, vision and strategy to customers</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Continually learn new technology to build competitive knowledge, technical skill, and credibility</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Deliver product and technical presentations with potential clients</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Have a direct line of communication with the product team to collaborate on feature requests</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Help clients onboard the product and assist when they run into roadblocks.&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Think creatively about a wide variety of technical challenges during the pre-sales life cycle&nbsp;</span></li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Knowledgeable and experienced with DevOps monitoring or architecture tools.&nbsp;</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Comfortable and confident in delivering technical presentations/demos to either external customers or internal teams</span></li> <li style="font-weight: 400;"><span style="font-weight: 400;">Able to think creatively about a wide variety of challenges. jobs.title: Sales Revenue Analyst - NYC
jobs.description: <p><strong>The Team:</strong></p> <p>Datadog’s Revenue Operations &amp; Finance team continues to expand as we work continuously to improve and solve business problems.</p> <p>The Sales Revenue team supports Datadog’s rapid growth by collaborating with teams across the organization, providing commercial, operational, and analytical support to ensure that Datadog's business and Sales organization continues to grow as rapidly and efficiently as possible.</p> <p>In this role, you will gain hands-on experience managing commission processes at scale while collaborating cross-functionally with Finance, Sales, Strategy, and Operations. This is an excellent opportunity for anyone seeking high visibility, meaningful ownership, and a clear path for growth within a fast-paced SaaS environment.</p> <p>We are looking for a Sales Revenue Analyst to join us in Boston, MA or New York, NY to support our team in managing global sales team compensation calculations and new business revenue recognition.</p> <p><strong>You Will:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Own the monthly sales commission process across multiple sales segments, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and operational excellence.</li> <li>Improve day-to-day operations by identifying process gaps, fixing inefficiencies, and building practical tracking for new initiatives as the business grows.</li> <li>Analyze compensation results to identify trends, surface risks, and provide actionable insights to leadership</li> <li>Review deal structures and financial terms to ensure alignment with compensation policies and revenue recognition requirements</li> <li>Partner cross-functionally with Finance, Sales, Systems and Strategy to support compensation plan execution and business initiatives</li> <li>Maintain and optimize compensation systems, reporting and dashboarding by leveraging tools such as Salesforce and CaptivateIQ to elevate sales performance visibility, improve data integrity, and enhance the overall experience for sales teams and cross-functional stakeholders</li> <li>Partner closely with Datadog’s most senior leaders to evaluate, design, and operationalize new sales strategy programs&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>You Are:</strong></p> <p><strong>Job Specific:</strong></p> <ul> <li>2-3 years of experience in Finance, Sales Operations, Revenue Operations, Incentive Compensation, or Business Analytics</li> <li>Highly analytical with advanced Excel skills and experience working with large datasets</li> <li>A strong problem solver who can recognize patterns and translate data into insights</li> <li>Comfortable operating in a fast-paced, high-growth environment with shifting priorities</li> </ul> <p><strong>Soft Skills:&nbsp;</strong></p> <ul> <li>Demonstrated experience thinking strategically about complex issues&nbsp;</li> <li>Excellent interpersonal skills with experience presenting to teams of various functions and levels</li> <li>Project ownership and delivery - work closely with cross-functional teams to manage stakeholders and coordinate, plan and prioritize tasks&nbsp;</li> </ul> <p><strong>Bonus Points:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Experience with SaaS company economics, deal structuring, and monthly recurring revenue business model</li> <li>Experience working with compensation &amp; business automation tools (such as CaptivateIQ)</li> <li>Experience in a high-growth<strong> </strong>SaaS company and/or multi-national public company</li> <li>Familiarity with Tableau, Metabase, and (or) other business intelligence systems</li> <li>Experience in sales-facing role or working with C-suite executives</li> </ul> <p><strong>About Us:</strong></p> <p>Datadog is the monitoring and security platform for cloud applications. Our SaaS product is used by organizations of all sizes across a wide range of industries to enable digital transformation, cloud migration, and infrastructure monitoring of our customers’ entire technology stack, allowing for seamless collaboration and problem-solving among Dev, Ops and Security teams globally. Given the resilience of cloud technologies and importance placed today in digital operations and agility, Datadog continues to innovate and is well positioned for the long term.&nbsp;</p> <p style="color: white;">#LI-Hybrid</p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. jobs.title: Strategic Finance Associate - NYC
jobs.description: <p>Datadog's Strategic Finance team transforms data from Datadog and external sources into actionable analytical insights on a cross-departmental basis, to drive positive change at Datadog. Building bottom-up and top-down analysis about our products and business activity, this team delivers insights on longer-term company opportunities, forecasts, and goals. This team delivers industry and competitive insights to executives and business leaders, and delivers near-term insights by analyzing internal usage data. This team also partners cross-functionally to evaluate strategic business and go-to-market opportunities. The Strategic Finance Associate will report to the SVP, Investor Relations &amp; Strategic Finance, and will interact with colleagues in the finance team and across the company.</p> <p>We are seeking a Strategic Finance Associate to join Datadog. They will play an integral role in the data collection and transformation, analysis, delivery, and communication of material and information to internal stakeholders across multiple divisions. They will be instrumental in turning data into meaningful insights, crafting reports illuminating takeaways and action items from those insights, and communicating these across the company. By doing so, this team will drive positive change to help Datadog grow and scale our business.&nbsp;</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table. We operate as a hybrid workplace to ensure our Datadogs can create a work-life harmony that best fits them.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>What You’ll Do:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Use advanced analytics to study revenue, product / platform, customer usage, and penetration trends.</li> <li>Develop meaningful insights on those trends, and disseminate analytical insights and action items using dashboards, presentations and reports tuned to various stakeholders and their interests.</li> <li>Participate in transformation projects to improve processes, automate repetitive tasks, increase data quality, and enable more stakeholders with data insights</li> <li>Create deliverables around near-term insights and provide analytical support on forecasting and planning processes.</li> <li>Craft top-down and bottom-up analyses to deliver long-term insights on the business and growth opportunities</li> <li>Support the preparation of presentations</li> <li>Collaborate across the organization, including finance, sales ops, product, marketing, and sales.</li> <li>Support ad hoc analytical projects from senior management, finance, etc.</li> </ul> <p><strong>Who You Are:</strong></p> <ul> <li>Strong sense of ownership, intellectual curiosity, and an ability to think creatively and critically</li> <li>Strong understanding of the financials and metrics performance, as well as the ability to gain understanding the product &amp; market, to distill into accessible and informative reports and narratives</li> <li>Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Experience communicating data heavy insights to technical and non-technical audiences.</li> <li>Ability to create and derive analytical implications from complex analyses and forecasting models</li> <li>Ability to craft impactful visualizations, reports, and presentations, making analyses&nbsp;</li> <li>BA/BS in Data Science, Computer Science/Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Economics, or other quantitative fields</li> <li>At least 1-3 years of professional experience, likely as a business analyst, data analyst, or in a quantitative finance or consulting role.</li> <li>Experience manipulating and analyzing complex datasets using SQL and/or Python.</li> <li>Proficiency with Excel</li> </ul> <p><em>Datadog values people from all walks of life. We understand not everyone will meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re passionate about technology and want to grow your skills, we encourage you to apply.</em></p> <p>&nbsp;</p> <p><strong>Benefits &amp; Growth:</strong>&nbsp;</p> <ul> <li>Generous and competitive benefits package</li> <li>New hire stock equity (RSUs) and employee stock purchase plan</li> <li>Continuous career development and pathing opportunities</li> <li>Employee-focused best in class onboarding</li> <li>Internal mentor and cross-departmental buddy program</li> <li>Friendly and inclusive workplace culture</li> </ul> <p><em>Benefits and Growth listed above may vary based on the country of your employment and the nature of your employment with Datadog.</em></p><div class="content-pay-transparency"><div class="pay-input"><div class="description"><p>Datadog offers a competitive salary and equity package, and may include variable compensation. PSEs engage with Datadog’s Premier Customers via standard technical support channels, but are also involved with cadence calls, demos/presentations, conferences, and various side projects. You will work directly with Datadog’s Premier Customer base, and will be immersed in a fast-paced environment where you will be challenged, but will also immediately witness your contributions to Datadog and to our customers.</p> <p><em>At Datadog, we place value in our office culture - the relationships and collaboration it builds and the creativity it brings to the table.
